jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.setup: Homer's iconic frustrated exclamation
jokes.punchline: The signature catchphrase itself
jokes.explanation: Homer's iconic catchphrase - perfect economy, character integration, and cultural impact. One word that defines the character.

jokes.text: I want to whip eggs at the warm-up act
jokes.setup: Barney's excitement about going to a concert
jokes.punchline: His bizarre plan to assault the opening act with eggs
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific absurdity for Barney, shows his crude concert-going approach

jokes.text: Wow, my concert-going jacket still fits. And this is where I used to hide my beer.
jokes.setup: Homer putting on his old concert jacket
jokes.punchline: Revealing it had a secret beer compartment
jokes.explanation: Nice character detail about Homer's past, the hidden beer pocket is a perfect Homer touch

jokes.text: Billy Beer!
jokes.setup: Homer finding old beer in his jacket
jokes.punchline: It's the notoriously bad Billy Carter beer from the 1970s
jokes.explanation: Great cultural reference - Billy Beer was a real product associated with Jimmy Carter's brother, perfect for the era

jokes.text: My little guy's first rock concert. I hope the Spinal Taps don't play too loud.
jokes.setup: Marge's motherly concern about the concert volume
jokes.punchline: Worrying about a band literally famous for playing absurdly loud
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge naivety - she doesn't understand that extreme volume is Spinal Tap's whole thing

jokes.text: Oh, I went to thousands of heavy metal concerts... and it never hurt me.
jokes.setup: Homer claiming metal concerts never hurt him
jokes.punchline: Obvious implication that his brain damage proves otherwise
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer obliviousness - the irony is perfect given his obvious mental state

jokes.text: I hear you.
jokes.setup: Homer's claim about metal concerts not hurting him
jokes.punchline: Marge's deadpan response suggesting she can't hear him properly
jokes.explanation: Subtle joke - Marge can't hear him, undermining his claim about hearing damage

jokes.text: There goes Davy Crockett... in his bald-skin cap.
jokes.setup: Homer spotting someone who looks like Davy Crockett
jokes.punchline: Mixing up 'coonskin cap' with 'bald-skin cap' due to the person's baldness
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay combining the famous coonskin cap reference with observational humor about baldness

jokes.text: ''Tapping'' into the ''spines'' of young voters, huh?
jokes.setup: News report about Mayor Quimby honoring Spinal Tap
jokes.punchline: Painfully obvious puns on the band's name
jokes.explanation: Weak joke - painfully obvious and forced puns that feel like lazy writing

jokes.text: Hey, Milhouse, cool jacket. It cost me 50,000 Bazooka Joe comics.
jokes.setup: Bart complimenting Milhouse's jacket
jokes.punchline: The absurdly high number of comics traded for it
jokes.explanation: Good kid logic absurdity - the specific reference to Bazooka Joe comics feels authentic to the character

jokes.text: Boy, some of the best times I've ever had... were in the back seat of a car.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining he won't be bored waiting in the car
jokes.punchline: Sexual innuendo that goes over Bart's head
jokes.explanation: Standard adult humor that works for the dual audience approach

jokes.text: Ohhh... Oh, baby.
jokes.setup: Homer getting comfortable in the car
jokes.punchline: His exaggerated pleasure at reclining the seat
jokes.explanation: Simple visual gag showing Homer's simple pleasures

jokes.text: Official tour '92 T-shirts, $31. Check it out-- Spinal Tap kicking Moammar Khadafy... in the butt. A timeless classic.
jokes.setup: Vendor selling overpriced concert merchandise
jokes.punchline: Absurd t-shirt design and calling it 'timeless' when it's clearly topical
jokes.explanation: Great satire of merchandise culture and the absurdity of calling topical political humor 'timeless'

jokes.text: After the Berlin Wall fell our records started selling... on the dismal side of the Iron Curtain. That gave us a boost. We're big in Bulgaria, and the other Garia.
jokes.setup: Spinal Tap explaining their recent success
jokes.punchline: 'Dismal side' instead of 'eastern side' and making up 'the other Garia'
jokes.explanation: Excellent character writing - captures Spinal Tap's mix of ignorance and confidence perfectly

jokes.text: Hungaria?
jokes.setup: Discussion of Eastern European countries
jokes.punchline: Mispronouncing Hungary as 'Hungaria'
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency - shows their geographic ignorance

jokes.text: Who's benefited... more from the death of communism than us? Maybe the people who live in communist countries.
jokes.setup: Spinal Tap claiming to be the biggest beneficiaries of communism's end
jokes.punchline: Reluctantly admitting the actual people might have benefited more
jokes.explanation: Brilliant satire of rock star ego and political obtuseness - the reluctant admission makes it perfect

jokes.text: But we each just bought our own soccer team. How many Hungarios can say that?
jokes.setup: Continuing the discussion about benefiting from communism's end
jokes.punchline: Making up the word 'Hungarios' for Hungarian people
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to the Hungary mispronunciation, showing consistent character ignorance

jokes.text: We don't know that. What if somebody rocks as good as you? Or better. We don't want to look stupid.
jokes.setup: Radio DJs asking for a promotional statement
jokes.punchline: Spinal Tap's pedantic refusal to make exaggerated claims
jokes.explanation: Perfect Spinal Tap characterization - their bizarre literal-mindedness about promotional claims

jokes.text: Well, I don't want to lie. Six days a week the place is a hockey rink.
jokes.setup: Complaint about water puddles on stage
jokes.punchline: Casually admitting the venue's dual use explains the problem
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan delivery of practical venue problems

jokes.text: Well, this is a rock concert... not the bleedin'... splish-splash show.
jokes.setup: Complaining about water on stage
jokes.punchline: The absurd phrase 'splish-splash show'
jokes.explanation: Good character voice - the British accent and absurd phrasing

jokes.text: Medium setting.
jokes.setup: Spinal Tap performing, presumably with their famous loud amplifiers
jokes.punchline: Claiming they're only on medium when it's clearly deafening
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to the film's famous 'these go to eleven' concept - economical and character-perfect

jokes.text: That's only four miles from my house!
jokes.setup: Band mentioning they drove down route 401
jokes.punchline: Random audience member getting excited about the geographic proximity
jokes.explanation: Nice touch of crowd psychology - how people get excited about random local connections

jokes.text: Well, it seems some silly twit... did not get a big enough oxygen pump... but that's supposed to be a devil. Filled with air, it's very evil and impressive. We salute you, our half-inflated dark lord!
jokes.setup: Stage prop (inflatable devil) failing during performance
jokes.punchline: Trying to maintain dignity while acknowledging the pathetic deflated prop
jokes.explanation: Brilliant physical comedy combined with perfect character voice - the pompous language about a failing inflatable is perfect Spinal Tap

jokes.text: People think there are only five members of Spinal Tap... but they're wrong. Could we turn up the house lights? That was the cue to turn up the lights... so we can tell the audience... they're the sixth member of the freakin' group!
jokes.setup: Trying to do a crowd participation bit
jokes.punchline: The technical failure makes the attempt at audience connection awkward
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy - the awkwardness of explaining a failed crowd interaction

jokes.text: Aah! My vision. That's it. Show's over. Good night, Springden. There will be no encores.
jokes.setup: Laser effects during the show
jokes.punchline: Getting blinded by their own effects and mispronouncing Springfield
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of concert disasters, the mispronunciation adds perfect insult to injury

jokes.text: They were only on for 20 minutes. What a gyp.
jokes.setup: Concert ending abruptly
jokes.punchline: Bart's practical disappointment about the short show
jokes.explanation: Good kid perspective - practical concern about value for money

jokes.text: Tonight the city weeps... as for the first time ever... a hockey arena becomes the scene of violence... following a concert by Spinal Tap.
jokes.setup: News report about concert violence
jokes.punchline: Acting like violence at a hockey arena is unprecedented
jokes.explanation: Brilliant irony - treating a hockey arena as a peaceful venue that's been corrupted by rock music

jokes.text: It would be wrong to suggest... this sort of mayhem began with rock and roll. After all, there were riots... at the premiere of Mozart's The Magic Flute. So, what's the answer-- ban all music? In this reporter's opinion, the answer is yes.
jokes.setup: Trying to provide balanced perspective on concert violence
jokes.punchline: Escalating to banning all music including classical
jokes.explanation: Masterful escalation from reasonable to absurd - the Mozart reference elevates it, then the extreme conclusion is perfect

jokes.text: I meant a real guitar. This is real.
jokes.setup: Marge suggesting they buy Bart a guitar
jokes.punchline: Homer's defensive response about their clearly inadequate guitar
jokes.explanation: Good visual setup implying they have a terrible guitar, Homer's defensiveness is in character

jokes.text: We spent a lot of money... so you get real good, real fast, or pow.!
jokes.setup: Homer explaining Bart's new guitar
jokes.punchline: Threatening violence if Bart doesn't improve quickly
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer parenting - turning encouragement into a threat

jokes.text: I'd like to play me latest chart topper. It's called... ''Me Fans Are Stupid Pigs.''
jokes.setup: Bart introducing his song
jokes.punchline: The insulting title and fake British accent
jokes.explanation: Good character development - Bart adopting rock star attitude and contempt for audience

jokes.text: Slag off. You've changed, man. It used to be about the music. I said slag off!.
jokes.setup: Lisa trying to talk to Bart about his music
jokes.punchline: Bart's rock star persona dismissing his sister
jokes.explanation: Good sibling dynamic with Bart's new persona creating conflict

jokes.text: Hey, Simpson... what are you trying to play? ''Polly-wolly Doodle.'' Oh, yeah? Well, it sounds Polly-wolly crappy.
jokes.setup: Kids mocking Bart's guitar playing
jokes.punchline: Turning the song title into an insult
jokes.explanation: Decent kid-level wordplay, simple but effective structure

jokes.text: Burn.
jokes.setup: Someone delivering an insult to Bart
jokes.punchline: Milhouse's single-word reaction acknowledging the burn
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and character voice for Milhouse commenting on social dynamics

jokes.text: That's all I did in high school. My old man said I was wasting my time... and I'd never amount to anything. I, uh... Hmm.
jokes.setup: Otto explaining his guitar skills
jokes.punchline: Realizing mid-sentence that his father was right
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment - Otto's self-awareness hitting him mid-speech

jokes.text: We don't have seat belts. Uh... Well, then just try to go limp.
jokes.setup: Otto telling kids to fasten seat belts
jokes.punchline: His casual advice to just go limp instead
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about school bus safety, Otto's casual attitude toward danger is perfect

jokes.text: It's a miracle... no one was hurt. I stand on my record-- 1 5 crashes and not a single fatality.
jokes.setup: Otto after crashing the bus
jokes.punchline: Being proud of only having 15 crashes with no deaths
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character comedy - Otto's twisted sense of professional pride in his terrible safety record

jokes.text: Let's see your license, pal. No can do. Never got one. If you need proof of my identity... I wrote my name on my underwear. Oh, wait-- these aren't mine.
jokes.setup: Police asking for Otto's license
jokes.punchline: Multiple revelations: no license, name in underwear, not his underwear
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation with each revelation getting worse - Otto's logic and lifestyle perfectly captured

jokes.text: Where's Otto? That's one palindrome you won't be hearing for a while.
jokes.setup: Bart asking where Otto is
jokes.punchline: Lisa pointing out Otto's name is a palindrome
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Lisa moment - smart wordplay that most viewers wouldn't catch immediately

jokes.text: Nonsense. One of our good citizens will slow down and wave me in. Nope.
jokes.setup: Grandpa's optimistic view of other drivers
jokes.punchline: Immediately being proven wrong
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective setup/payoff about driving courtesy

jokes.text: I was thinking about the time... Homer caught his nose in the toaster. We'll watch the tape tonight.
jokes.setup: Kids laughing on the bus
jokes.punchline: Absurd explanation and that they have it recorded
jokes.explanation: Great absurd Homer incident - nose in toaster is perfectly ridiculous

jokes.text: My name's Patty. I'll be testing you. When you do good, I use the green pen. When you do bad, I use the red pen. Any questions?
jokes.setup: Patty explaining the driving test procedure
jokes.punchline: Her matter-of-fact, humorless approach
jokes.explanation: Good character establishment for Patty's bureaucratic personality

jokes.text: Yeah, one: Have you always been a chick? No offense, but you were born a man, weren't you? You can tell me. I'm open-minded.
jokes.setup: Patty asking if Otto has questions
jokes.punchline: Otto's inappropriate and clueless question about her appearance
jokes.explanation: Peak Otto cluelessness - inappropriate question delivered with complete innocence

jokes.text: Well, you failed every segment... and misspelled ''bus'' on your application. Drag.
jokes.setup: Otto taking driving test
jokes.punchline: Failing completely including misspelling the basic word
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - not only failing the test but basic literacy. Otto's casual 'Drag' response is perfect

jokes.text: All I found in there was a jar of mustard... and a couple of old Cycle magazines. Wow! I had mustard?
jokes.setup: Landlord listing Otto's meager possessions
jokes.punchline: Otto being excited about having mustard
jokes.explanation: Perfect Otto moment - his excitement about mustard shows how little he has and how oblivious he is

jokes.text: Otto man? You're living in a Dumpster? Oh, man, I wish. Dumpster brand trash bins are top-of-the-line. This is just a Trash-co waste disposal unit.
jokes.setup: Bart finding Otto living in what looks like a dumpster
jokes.punchline: Otto's brand consciousness about garbage containers
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - Otto maintaining brand standards even while homeless. The absurd product placement parody is perfect.

jokes.text: Mom, I thought... you might forget... our conversation this afternoon... so I took the precaution of recording it. What conversation? Mom, can Otto live in our garage? He sure can.
jokes.setup: Bart playing a recording to remind Marge
jokes.punchline: The obviously fake recording with Marge's voice
jokes.explanation: Good Bart scheme - the fake recording is obvious but creative

jokes.text: The admiral and I don't get along.
jokes.setup: Asked why he can't stay with his parents
jokes.punchline: Referring to his father as 'the admiral'
jokes.explanation: Nice character detail suggesting Otto's military/authoritarian father

jokes.text: But doesn't the Bible also say... ''Thou shalt not take... moochers into thy... hut''?
jokes.setup: Marge quoting the Bible about helping others
jokes.punchline: Homer's fake biblical quote against helping moochers
jokes.explanation: Excellent Homer logic - creating biblical authority for his selfishness with fake archaic language

jokes.text: The can of corn costs... 57 ¢! I could sure go for a can of corn.
jokes.setup: Homer complaining about grocery prices
jokes.punchline: Otto's longing for basic canned food
jokes.explanation: Good contrast showing Otto's low standards and Homer's complaints

jokes.text: Uh, you got any of those Where's Waldo? books? No. - Anything from the vampire's point of view? - No. Anything where guys... send in naked pictures of their chicks?
jokes.setup: Otto asking for reading material
jokes.punchline: Escalating from children's books to inappropriate magazines
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Otto's limited intellectual range and inappropriate requests

jokes.text: Of course I'm not mad. If something's hard to do, then it's not worth doing. You just stick that guitar in the closet... next to your shortwave radio... your karate outfit, and your unicycle... and we'll go inside and watch TV.
jokes.setup: Homer responding to Bart quitting guitar
jokes.punchline: His anti-motivational philosophy and list of Bart's abandoned hobbies
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Homer parenting philosophy - completely backwards life lesson delivered with confidence. The list of abandoned hobbies is perfect.

jokes.text: Hey, Pop-N-Fresh! You're supposed to giggle. Gyyaaagh!!
jokes.setup: Otto poking Homer's belly expecting Pillsbury Doughboy reaction
jokes.punchline: Homer's aggressive response to being poked
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy combining cultural reference with Homer's irritability

jokes.text: Did the maniac kill her? Natch. You know how I know? How? Because... I was that maniac.
jokes.setup: Otto telling a scary story with Lisa asking questions
jokes.punchline: Otto claiming to be the killer from the urban legend
jokes.explanation: Good scary story subversion with Otto's inappropriate revelation

jokes.text: This is not Happy Days, and he is not the Fonz. Hey-y-y, Mr. S.
jokes.setup: Homer rejecting the family's attachment to Otto
jokes.punchline: Otto immediately doing a Fonzie impression
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing - Otto immediately proving Homer's point by acting exactly like the Fonz

jokes.text: There's plenty of money out there... for a guy who knows how to fake his own death.
jokes.setup: Otto asked what he'll do for money
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning insurance fraud as a career option
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor showing Otto's casual attitude toward crime

jokes.text: Wow. I've never been called an adult before. I've been tried as one, but...
jokes.setup: Bart calling Otto the coolest adult he's met
jokes.punchline: Otto revealing he's been tried as an adult in court
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor - Otto's criminal past delivered as casual conversation. Perfect economy and character insight.

jokes.text: ''Alcohol increases your ability to drive.'' False?! Oh, man!
jokes.setup: Otto taking the written driving test
jokes.punchline: His shock that alcohol doesn't improve driving
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Otto's backwards understanding of basic safety

jokes.text: He didn't call you a bum. He called you a sponge. Sponge?! Does this look like something a sponge would do?! I'll show him... who's a sponge.
jokes.setup: Bart trying to motivate Otto by correcting Homer's insult
jokes.punchline: Otto getting more offended by 'sponge' than 'bum'
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Otto's pride being wounded by the specific insult

jokes.text: So I can staple my license... to Homer Simpson's big bald head!
jokes.setup: Otto explaining his motivation for retaking the test
jokes.punchline: His violent but absurd revenge plan
jokes.explanation: Good Otto revenge fantasy - specific and violent but ultimately harmless

jokes.text: Homer had a piece of food on his face for three days. It wasn't little either. It was a chicken wing.
jokes.setup: Kids discussing Homer's mishaps
jokes.punchline: The absurd detail about the size and duration of food on his face
jokes.explanation: Great absurd Homer detail - escalating from 'piece of food' to 'chicken wing' for three days

jokes.text: Yes, hail to the bus driver... bus driver man.
jokes.setup: Kids celebrating Otto's return
jokes.punchline: Singing a modified version of 'Hail to the Chief'
jokes.explanation: Nice ending celebration with simple musical parody
